reasoning: The question asks whether the number of 'Strategic developments' in Ethiopia for four 30-day periods will exceed the 360-day average of ~29.75. Recent reports from the Ethiopia Peace Observatory (ACLED) show a strong downward trend in conflict. Battles decreased by 44% in Amhara from mid-December 2024 to January 2025, and overall political violence events dropped by 58% in late February 2025 compared to the prior two weeks. Peace agreements (like the one with the OLA faction) have contributed to this decline. Strategic developments (which include arrests, looting, base establishments, and peace talks) are highly correlated with overall conflict intensity. Since violence is currently much lower than the 360-day average, the rate of strategic developments in the near term is highly likely to remain below the ~29.75 threshold. For more distant dates, uncertainty increases due to the potential for conflict to flare up again, so the probability regresses toward the base rate.
